commands.review_summary:
The PR forwards isolated cron `toolsAllow` and `senderIsOwner: true` into embedded and CLI agent execution paths and adds regression coverage for both paths.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main passes cron `toolsAllow` only to embedded runs, while the CLI runner only fails closed when that parameter is present. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor real-behavior proof plus maintainer acceptance of the owner-auth and fail-closed upgrade semantics; no narrow code repair is identified for automation.

target.title: fix(cron): authorize isolated tool allowlist runs
target.body:
## Summary
- Mark scheduled isolated cron executions as owner-authorized when invoking embedded or CLI agent backends
- Forward cron payload toolsAllow into the CLI backend path as well as embedded runs
- Add regression coverage for embedded and CLI cron runs using explicit tool allow-lists

## Verification
- pnpm vitest run src/cron/isolated-agent/run.tools-allow.test.ts
- pnpm vitest run src/cron/isolated-agent/run.tools-allow.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent.helpers.test.ts
- pnpm build
target.author: shad0wca7
